change_under_test: Added references/harness-inductive-bias.md (Zhang LID/compositional-generalization), docs/sources provenance file, and a body weave in SKILL.md framing positioning LID as the harness quality criterion.
note_on_metrics: Precise token/duration telemetry is not exposed to the run subagents; token/time values are self-reported approximations and should be treated as rough, not authoritative.

delta.targeted: +5 assertions (44% -> 100%) on the two prompts that probe long-horizon / long-input harness design
delta.control: no change (3/3 both); new content correctly NOT loaded for the support-bot prompt (0 new-vocab hits)

analyst_notes: Discriminating assertions are the LID/context-rot/equivalence-class ones (A24.1, A24.3, A25.1, A25.3, A25.5). The snapshot reaches comparable *practical* architectures (external store + sub-agents; map-reduce + retrieve) via different framings (memory-eviction; bottleneck-information principle), so the lift is in root-cause vocabulary and the degenerate-decomposition caveat, not in whether a workable design is produced.

Non-discriminating: fabrication-as-Verify (A24.4) and avoid-bigger-model (A25.4) passed in both configs regardless of the new reference — the existing guardrails/orchestration references already cover them.

Regression check clean: new-skill control run read only orchestration + guardrails refs and explicitly skipped harness-inductive-bias.md; 0 hits of new vocabulary. No token bloat attributable to the new reference on off-topic prompts.
Cost note surfaced by the reference (RLM 1.5-3x slower, wins at scale) was correctly reproduced in eval-25 with_skill, matching the source's honest-downside framing.
